texto: When all the teachers are gone, who will be your teacher?The student replied: “Everything!Kobun, paused, then said: “No, you".
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Kobun Chino Otogawa Roshi que provoca temas ligados a educacao. Ao questionar “When all the teachers are gone, who will be your teacher?The student replied: “Everything!Kobun, …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre educacao, learning, vida, com foco em vida e self.

frase_original: When all the teachers are gone, who will be your teacher?The student replied: “Everything!Kobun, paused, then said: “No, you".

texto: We sit to make life meaningful. The significance of our life is not experienced in striving to create some perfect thing. We must simply start with accepting ourselves. Sitting brings us back to actually who and where we are. This can be very painful. Self-acceptance is the hardest thing to do. If we can’t accept ourselves, we are living in ignorance, this darkest night. We may still be awake, but we don’t know where we are. We cannot see. The mind has no light. Practice is this candle in our very darkest room.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Kobun Chino Otogawa Roshi sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “We sit to make life meaningful. The significance of our life is not experienced in striving to cr…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, vida, self, útil em momentos de reflexao-noturna e boa-noite.

Sitting brings us back to actually who and where we are. This can be very painful. Self-acceptance is the hardest thing to do. If we can’t accept ourselves, we are living in ignorance, this darkest night. We may still be awake, but we don’t know where we are. We cannot see. The mind has no light. Practice is this candle in our very darkest room.
